raw_consumer_statement: I am filing a complaint regarding Citibanks handling of a billing dispute for merchandise I did not receive. I purchased merchandise from XXXX totaling {$1200.00}. The merchant claims the package was delivered, but the only evidence provided is a photograph of the package left unattended in the lobby of my apartment building, a shared public space accessible to anyone. There was no signature confirmation, no hand-off to me, and no proof that I personally received the merchandise. Citibank initially issued a provisional credit while investigating. After the merchant submitted the lobby photo, Citibank reversed the credit and held me responsible for the charge, treating delivery to a building lobby as equivalent to delivery to me. I dispute this determination. Delivery to a shared lobby without signature or confirmation does not establish receipt by the cardholder. I never took possession of the merchandise and did not authorize or consent to an unsecured delivery method for high-value goods. The merchant chose to ship the package without requiring a signature and therefore assumed the risk of loss prior to receipt. Citibank has advised me to resolve this directly with the merchant ; however, XXXX has refused to refund me. As a result, I am being forced to pay for merchandise I never received. I believe Citibanks decision is inconsistent with fair dispute-resolution standards and improperly shifts the merchants delivery risk to the consumer without proof of receipt.
